data.title: Gold slips to three-week low as Middle East war muddles rate outlook
data.description:
Gold futures fell for the fourth time in five sessions on Friday, closing below $5,100/oz, pressured by a stronger dollar, rising oil prices, and uncertainty around Federal Reserve policy.

The dollar flirted this week with its strongest levels YTD as surging energy prices sparked worries about Europe's import-dependent economy and drove investors toward ‌the safety of the greenback, making dollar-priced bullion less affordable for other currency holders.

The dollar "has strengthened due to its safe-haven status, particularly as rising oil prices benefit the U.S., which is a net energy exporter," ANZ analysts said in a note. "Concerns are mounting over the Fed's approach to rate cuts, with higher oil prices raising the possibility of renewed inflation pressures."

Data showed U.S. consumer spending increased slightly more than expected in January, which combined with continued strength in underlying inflation and the Iran war, sustained views by economists that the Federal Reserve would not resume cutting ​interest rates for some ​time.

The gold price remains unable to benefit from the Middle East crisis, Commerzbank commodity analyst Barbara Lambrecht said in a note. With Brent crude above $100/bbl, inflation risks are rising, clouding the outlook for U.S. rate cuts, but "it is also likely that investors are trying to cover losses in other asset classes, as outflows from gold ETFs have resumed since the beginning of March."

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for March delivery closed -1.2% on Friday to $5,052.50/oz, while front-month Comex March silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended -4.4% to $80.914/oz, the lowest settlement value for both metals since February 19; for the full week, gold dropped 1.8% and silver slumped 3.5%.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: OceanPal receives Nasdaq non-compliance notice
data.description:
• OceanPal (SVRN) said on Friday that it has received a Nasdaq notice indicating that the company's common shares failed to maintain a minimum closing bid price of $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive business days from January 29, 2026, through March 12, 2026.
  • The notice said that the company is ineligible for a compliance period due to a prior one-for-twenty-five (1-for-25) reverse stock split effected on August 25, 2025.
  • The company said that it intends to timely request a hearing before the panel.
  • The company said its board of directors is actively evaluating all available measures to restore compliance with the Minimum Bid Price Requirement, including potential capital markets transactions and other corporate actions within the board's existing shareholder-authorized authority.
  • SVRN +1.84% after hours to $0.3581.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Azitra receives notice of non-compliance with NYSE American Listing standards
data.description:
• Azitra (AZTR) was notified by NYSE American that it does not meet minimum stockholders’ equity requirements that requires at least $6M in stockholders’ equity for companies with losses in the past five fiscal years.
  • Azitra previously received a non-compliance notice in October 2025 and submitted a plan to regain compliance, which was accepted December 16, 2025.
  • The company has until April 1, 2027 to regain compliance; failure to do so may result in delisting proceedings.
  • AZTR shares down 4.6% post-market.

data.title: Lennar targets 85,000 home deliveries for 2026 while driving margin recovery through cost and tech initiatives
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Lennar Corporation (LEN) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Stuart Miller, Executive Chairman & CEO, opened the call by acknowledging the challenging housing market, highlighting intensified volatility due to Middle East events and a pullback in institutional purchasers, but asserted, “we believe that we are closer to an inflection point for Lennar than at any time in the past 3 years.” Miller emphasized adaptation over waiting for market recovery, noting ongoing improvements in the asset-light, land-light platform and cost structure.
  • Miller stated, “Our gross margin in the first quarter was 15.2%, reflecting improving discipline across construction, land and overhead. Our SG&A came in at 9.8%, slightly above expectations, net margin was 5.3%, producing net income of $229 million and EPS of $0.93.”
  • The company started 17,425 homes and sold 18,515 homes, maintaining inventory discipline. The average sales price was $374,000, with sales incentives on deliveries at 14.1%.
  • Miller highlighted major management changes, announcing Jon Jaffe’s retirement and the promotion of Jim Parker and David Grove to Area Presidents, stating, “Jon felt it was a good time to retire and frankly, Jim and David were ready and anxious for their term at that. They are tenured. They are proven Lennar professionals and they are energized by the opportunity.”
  • Jim Parker, Area President of Homebuilding & Land, described the company’s strategy as “refining products, optimizing our Everything's Included packages, rebuilding margins and using mortgage rate buydown to maintain or regain momentum.” Parker reported that Lennar is the #1 builder by market share in 22 of the top 50 homebuilding markets.
  • David Grove, Area President of Homebuilding & Land, noted a 2.5% reduction in direct construction costs from Q4 and an 11% year-over-year reduction in cycle time for single-family detached homes, now at 122 days, an all-time low for Lennar.
  • Diane Bessette, CFO & VP, stated, “For the first quarter, our Financial Services team had operating earnings of $91 million. The lower earnings were mainly derived from our mortgage business. The decrease was primarily based on the mix of buy-down programs offered to our Homebuilding divisions, including an increase in ARMs versus fixed-rate mortgages with ARMs generating significantly lower earnings.” Bessette also emphasized the company’s strong balance sheet with $2.1 billion in cash and a homebuilding debt-to-capital ratio of 15.7%.

Outlook

  • Bessette provided Q2 2026 guidance: new orders between 21,000 and 22,000 homes, deliveries between 20,000 and 21,000 homes, average sales price between $370,000 and $375,000, and gross margin in the 15.5% to 16% range. She added, “we believe our Q2 -- our Q1 margin of 15.2% should represent the low point for the year.” EPS for Q2 is expected in the range of $1.10 to $1.40. Full year delivery target remains at 85,000 homes.

Financial Results

  • Lennar reported net income of $229 million and EPS of $0.93. Gross margin was 15.2% and SG&A was 9.8%. The company ended the quarter with $2.1 billion in cash, inventory turn of 2.5x, and a return on inventory of 17.4%. Community count was 1,678, up 6% year-over-year.
  • Financial Services operating earnings came in at $91 million, impacted by a shift toward ARMs in the mortgage mix. Homebuilding inventory stood at $10.5 billion, and less than 5% of land remained on balance sheet.

Q&A

  • Alan Ratner, Zelman & Associates LLC: Asked about trends in incentives, rate buydown costs, and the impact on margin guidance. Miller responded, “As we sit today...we're not seeing significant movement either in traffic or in the ability to sell,” with Parker and Grove confirming steady demand patterns.
  • Ratner also questioned SG&A trends and expectations for the rest of the year. Miller replied, “...we are seeing opportunities and expectations that our overhead is going to be meaningfully lower as we come to the end of the year, whether it actually flows through one quarter or another, we're going to wait and see.”
  • Stephen Kim, Evercore ISI: Inquired about market share versus volume focus. Miller explained, “We don't have a specific mandate to grow market share, but we do recognize that with advantaged market share, we are able to work with trade partners and landholders to do a better job of negotiating.”
  • Susan Maklari, Goldman Sachs: Asked about inventory turn and the role of core products. Miller and Grove highlighted progress on cycle time and increased use of core plans, with Grove stating, “That's generally across the platform, call it, 65% core, and that's going to vary by division from some at 50% to some at 90%.”
  • John Lovallo, UBS: Queried cash flow pressures and delivery targets. Bessette attributed lower cash flow to average sales price decreases, while Miller and Parker cited optimism about execution and associate engagement.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral to inquisitive tone, focusing on margin guidance, cost controls, and volume strategy. No overt skepticism or negativity was detected, and questions were grounded in operational specifics.
  • Management’s tone was confident during prepared remarks and consistently constructive in Q&A, frequently referencing optimism, strategic adaptation, and cost discipline. Miller’s use of “we are certain” and “we are building toward it deliberately and with confidence” reflected this stance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts showed slightly greater confidence in cost and technology initiatives, while still acknowledging ongoing market uncertainty.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for gross margin in Q2 2026 is 15.5% to 16%, slightly improved from the 15% to 16% Q1 guidance from the previous call. EPS guidance for Q2 is higher than Q1’s $0.80 to $1.10 range.
  • Community count grew 6% year-over-year in Q1 2026, versus an 18% year-over-year growth noted at the end of Q4 2025.
  • Cycle time improved from 127 days in Q4 2025 to 122 days in Q1 2026. Inventory turn increased from 2.2x to 2.5x quarter-over-quarter.
  • Management’s tone shifted from cautious optimism in Q4 to a more proactive, opportunity-driven stance in Q1, emphasizing technology and operational improvements as drivers for recovery.
  • The retirement of Jon Jaffe and appointment of new Area Presidents mark a significant leadership transition this quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited persistent affordability challenges, volatile interest rates, and global uncertainties, especially the Middle East conflict, as ongoing risks.
  • Miller pointed to supply chain cost pressures from tariffs and immigration issues, stating, “the cost structure in the industry is pushing higher and is difficult to manage.”
  • The sidelining of institutional purchasers was flagged as a factor that could reduce demand and impact supply decisions.
  • Management outlined mitigation strategies through technology upgrades, cost controls, and maintaining flexibility in market approach.

Final Takeaway

Lennar’s management underscored a strong commitment to maintaining volume, driving down costs, and refining its asset-light, technology-forward operating model in the face of ongoing market headwinds. With a full-year delivery target of 85,000 homes and a focus on operational efficiency, the company believes it is positioned for rapid margin recovery and long-term growth as market conditions stabilize and demand reactivates.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: InflaRx receives Nasdaq notification
data.description:
• InflaRx (IFRX) said on Friday it had received a notice from Nasdaq for failing to meet the $1 minimum bid price requirement after its shares closed below that level for 30 consecutive business days.

  • The company had been granted 180 calendar days, until Sept. 7, 2026, to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.

  • The company said it may apply to transfer its listing from the Nasdaq Global Select Market to the Nasdaq Capital Market, which could give it an additional 180 days, until March 8, 2027, to regain compliance with the minimum bid price rule.
  • Shares -3.56%.

data.title: Stocks to watch after the bell on Friday: PSKY, SNEX, CCJ
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes ended lower on Friday as investors digested a fresh batch of economic data, highlighted by the latest PCE inflation and GDP reports.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) ended -0.6%, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) closed -0.2%, and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) finished -0.9%.

Here are the three stocks to watch on the day:

Paramount Skydance (PSKY) drew attention in after-hours trade following reports that the National Football League is in discussions with CBS to extend its Sunday football broadcasting deal through the 2033-2034 season. The proposed agreement would include a $1 billion increase over the current contract, bringing the total price tag to approximately $3.1 billion, according to sources cited by CNBC. In exchange for the higher price, the NFL would eliminate an opt-out clause after the 2029-2030 season that exists in the original deal. Paramount President Jeffrey Shell said during a recent earnings call that the company feels “pretty confident” it will remain in business with the NFL for a long time and has properly accounted for the expected impact of the negotiations in its internal forecasts.

StoneX Group (SNEX) moved after a FINRA arbitration panel ordered the company and its unit, StoneX Financial, to pay approximately $1.8 million in net monetary damages to BTIG. The ruling stems from a 2023 civil complaint related to StoneX’s employment of certain former BTIG employees. While the panel found BTIG liable for roughly $1.0 million in compensatory damages to StoneX Financial, it determined StoneX was liable for a $2.9 million payment to BTIG, with the final liability reflecting an offset of both sums.

Cameco (CCJ) closed down 6.4% on Friday to its lowest level in two months following a report that the Trump administration is exploring alternatives to Westinghouse in its push to revive the U.S. nuclear industry. According to Canary Media, the Department of Energy has held talks with executives from GE Vernova Hitachi Nuclear Energy (GEV) and South Korean diplomats representing Korea Electric Power (KEP) to discuss potential financing for new large reactors. Cameco owns a 49% interest in Westinghouse, whose majority owner Brookfield Asset Management (BAM) has reportedly struggled to reach a deal with the administration over concerns that federal financing options do not include cost-overrun insurance.

data.title: Jayud Global Logistics announces pricing of $6.7M registered direct offering
data.description:
• Jayud Global Logistics (JYD) on Friday said it has entered into a securities purchase agreement with several investors for the purchase and sale of 5,025,000 Class A ordinary shares at a purchase price of $1.34 per share in a registered direct offering.
  • The gross proceeds to the company from this offering are expected to be approximately $6.73 million.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about March 17, 2026.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for general corporate purposes, including working capital and the expansion of its overseas business operations.
  • JYD -12.37% after hours to $2.55.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cameco slumps as Trump administration reportedly courts Westinghouse rivals in nuclear talks
data.description:
Cameco (CCJ) closed -6.4% on Friday to the lowest in two months following a report that slow-moving talks with Westinghouse, the developer of the flagship U.S. nuclear reactor, have prompted the Trump administration to explore alternatives in its push to revive the U.S. nuclear industry.

According to Canary Media, the U.S. Department of Energy has held talks in recent weeks with executives from GE Vernova Hitachi Nuclear Energy (GEV) (HTHIY) (HTHIF) and South Korean diplomats representing state-owned Korea Electric Power (KEP) to discuss potential financing if either company decides to compete with Westinghouse to build new large reactors.

The talks developed as the Trump administration reportedly struggles to reach a deal with Westinghouse majority owner Brookfield Asset Management (BAM), as the U.S. government's generous financing options for new reactors nevertheless do not include cost-overrun insurance; Cameco (CCJ) owns a 49% interest in Westinghouse.

data.title: NFL negotiating new $3B deal with Paramount for Sunday night football
data.description:
The National Football League is reportedly in discussions with Paramount Skydance (PSKY) for CBS to continue broadcasting Sunday football games through the 2033 to 2034 season for a $1B increase over its current contract.

That would put the price tag at around $3.1B, according to sources cited by CNBC.

For the higher price, the NFL would eliminate the opt-out clause after the 2029-2030 season that is in the original deal with Paramount (PSKY). This would allow the football league to end the contract earlier, similar to options it has with Comcast (CMCSA), Amazon Prime Video (AMZN), and Fox (FOXA).  

“We feel pretty confident we're going to be in business with the NFL for a long time, and we have properly accounted for what we expect to be whatever impact of that negotiation in our kind of internal forecast going forward,” Paramount President Jeffrey Shell said during the company’s earnings call with analysts last month.

data.title: Wallbox receives NYSE non-compliance notice
data.description:
• Wallbox N.V. (WBX) announced on Friday that it received the non-compliance letter from the New York Stock Exchange, indicating that the company is not currently in compliance with the NYSE Listed Company Manual, which requires listed companies to maintain an average global market capitalization of at least $50 million over a consecutive 30 trading-day period and stockholders’ equity of at least $50 million.
  • Under applicable NYSE rules, the company must notify the NYSE within 30 business days confirming receipt of the non-compliance letter and indicating whether it intends to submit a plan to regain compliance.
  • According to the notice, the company will have up to 90 days from receipt of the Non-Compliance Letter to present definitive actions designed to restore compliance with the applicable continued listing standards within the prescribed cure period, which may extend up to 18 months.
  • The company said it intends to work with the NYSE and evaluate its available options to address the matter and regain compliance. 
  • Source: Press Release
